methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Biobot Analytics, a Series B biotech company founded by Mariana Matus and Newsha Ghaeli, has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, though public-company multiples cannot be applied without revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $40M raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-C progression, implies a valuation of $76M. Peer-median anchoring across five same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $810M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous risk profiles and technology maturity within the peer cohort.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $65M to $931M, with a median of $443M. The substantial width reflects material uncertainty about Biobot's competitive positioning and market opportunity relative to peers. Stage-progression provides a floor grounded in capital deployed; peer medians reflect market pricing for comparable development-stage assets. Key sensitivities include the company's revenue trajectory, IP defensibility, and regulatory pathway—factors that would narrow valuation confidence at later funding stages.
